description: Slate — Random Labs orchestration AI. Five primitives: read, write, grep, terminal, websearch. All agentcore access via terminal (curl, colgrep, cat).
notes: Slate has no config dir, no file-deploy mechanism. Bridge is a rules file auto-loaded into context. All toolshed access is terminal-mediated. sync.py not applicable.

agent_file.native_name: System prompt (injected at instantiation, not a file Slate reads)
agent_file.note: AGENTS.md content is surfaced via ~/.claude/rules/ auto-load. Slate cannot directly read its own system prompt file, but rules files serve the same purpose.

rules.native_name: Rules files auto-loaded from ~/.claude/rules/*.md at session start
rules.note: Slate's rules are auto-loaded by the Claude Code harness layer. agentcore-slate.md is the bridge rule.

skills.native_name: On-demand skill injection via terminal (cat SKILL.md into thread prompt)
skills.note: Slate reads skill files via terminal and injects content into worker thread prompts. No auto-load — Slate must explicitly read and include skill content.

commands.native_name: Command markdown files read on-demand via terminal
commands.note: No slash command system. Slate reads command .md files and executes the procedures manually. Functionally equivalent but not automatic.

custom_tools.native_name: terminal primitive — curl, colgrep, bun, git, bash scripts
custom_tools.note: Anything on PATH or invocable via shell is available. Executor at port 8000 provides TypeScript runtime. Full CLI access.

hooks.note: Slate has no event hooks. Session start/end orientation is handled via /starting-session skill executed manually.

mcp_servers.native_name: HTTP MCP via curl through terminal primitive
mcp_servers.note: No native MCP client. Slate calls MCP endpoints via curl in terminal. Stateless per call — no session persistence. executor (8000) and subagent-mcp (3096) are highest-value targets.

subagents.native_name: Worker threads (Slate's native parallelism model) + subagent-mcp delegation
subagents.note: Slate spawns isolated worker threads natively. For your defined subagents (reviewer, test-writer, architect, debugger, sigil-distiller), Slate delegates via subagent-mcp HTTP. Worker threads can also curl subagent-mcp directly.

plugins.note: No plugin architecture. Extended capability comes via executor (port 8000) TS runtime.
